channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ruby completes switch to Prism parser
item.description: Ruby 3.4.0, and Ruby 3.4.1, updates to the longstanding dynamic, open source programming language, have arrived, with Prism now the default parser. Ruby 3.4.0 was released December 25, Christmas Day, along with an update, Ruby 3.4.1, which merely changed the version number of the release. Ruby 3.4.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Your new AirTag has a hidden warning that's just for kids – and it's for the best
item.description: Apple's AirTag is now in compliance with US's Reese's Law, according to the government's consumer product safety watchdog.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: No surprise: AI, cyber continued to dominate in 2024
item.description: Current and former federal technology executives weighed in on what topics drove the storylines in 2024. The post No surprise: AI, cyber continued to dominate in 2024 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: California begins 2025 with solid start to winter snowpack, but more storms are needed
item.description: California is beginning 2025 with a solid start to the winter snowpack, but more storms are needed to keep the state’s water supplies on track.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Americans more likely to embrace driverless cars, but still skeptical: Survey
item.description: Americans are more likely to embrace driverless cars but are still skeptical of them, according to a new survey from YouGov. In the survey, 35 percent of Americans said when it comes to driverless cars’ advancement, they are “very enthusiastic” or “somewhat enthusiastic.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Tesla's Cybertruck Qualifies for Up to $7,500 in US Tax Credits
item.description: Tesla's Cybertruck qualifies for up to $7,500 in US tax credits this year, part of reshuffling under tougher rules that also disqualify other electric vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft claims Edge had a great year, boasts soaring browser user numbers
item.description: Microsoft claims 10 billion GenAI chats happened from within Edge, but browser still lags behind Chrome.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Windsurf Cascade uses AI to help beginners code games and applications
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. I don’t know how to code.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: AI, power availability and Intel’s future
item.description: As 2025 kicks off, it’s time to dust off the crystal ball. Here are my data center predictions for the coming year. 1) Trump backs off on tech tariffs For all the handwringing, Trump has a history of not following through on his threats.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump blames ‘open borders’ for New Orleans, Las Vegas attacks
item.description: President-elect Trump blamed the situation at the southern border for the recent attacks in New Orleans and Las Vegas, railing against President Biden for his immigration policies as both cases are being investigated.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S25 could borrow two great features from the Pixel 9 Pro
item.description: The next Samsung flagship could lift a couple of neat features from the Pixel 9 Pro, such as crash detection and a free trial of Gemini Advanced.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: How a flying ferry could help solve Tahoe’s traffic problem
item.description: "Hydrofoil" technology would transport passengers across Lake Tahoe in a half hour, potentially reducing traffic.
item.dc:creator: Lisa M. Krieger, The Mercury News

item.title: I'm raising my bread-making game in 2025 with these 5 affordable gadgets and apps
item.description: These handy kitchen gadgets will help you become the ultimate bread baker in 2025 – without using a bread maker.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nationwide boss says women working at home could be missing out on job opportunities
item.description: British bank says working from home is great for flexibility, but it doesn’t give you the best career opportunities.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Gen AI in 2025: Playtime is over, time to get practical
item.description: Generative AI playtime may be over, as organizations cut down on experimentation and pivot toward achieving business value, with a focus on fewer, more targeted use cases.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Fine-tuning Azure OpenAI models in Azure AI Foundry
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) like the OpenAI models used by Azure are general-purpose tools for building many different types of generative AI-powered applications, from chatbots to agent-powered workflows.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Vehicle Rental App Turo Involved in Deadly Attacks on New Year's Day
item.description: Deadly attacks on New Year's Day involved vehicles rented through Turo, a peer-to-peer car rental app, raising questions about safety and security protocols.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about the car-renting app used in the Las Vegas and New Orleans attacks
item.description: The car-renting app Turo is in the spotlight after both the cars used in the New Orleans and Las Vegas attacks on New Year's Day were rented through the company.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: IRS deploys AI tools to combat emerging tech’s role in new fraud schemes
item.description: AI tools are accelerating the development of deepfake schemes and disinformation campaigns that led to fraud The IRS is trying to keep up. The post IRS deploys AI tools to combat emerging tech’s role in new fraud schemes first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Agencies required to share custom software under new law
item.description: The SHARE IT Act, now law, builds on previous efforts to ensure agencies are sharing common software code across government. The post Agencies required to share custom software under new law first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Leaked Galaxy S25 benchmarks apparently confirm an all-Snapdragon line-up for 2025
item.description: New benchmarks suggest all of the Galaxy S25 models across the globe are getting Snapdragon chipsets.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AI-powered robot sinks seemingly impossible basketball hoops
item.description: A humanoid robot named CUE6 has got game. The basketball-playing robot, produced by Toyota, claimed a Guinness World Record for longest shot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
